          package main

          import (
        



          4
            "encoding/json"
        



          5
            "fmt"
        



          6
            "io"
        



          7
            "net/http"
        



          8
            "net/url"
        



          9
            "strings"
        



          10
          )
        



          11
          
        



          12
          func main() {
        



          13
            client := &http.Client{}
        



          14
            targetURL := "https://scraperapi.thordata.com/builder"
        



          15
          
        



          16
            spiderInfo := []map[string]string{ 
        



          17
              {
        



          18
                "url": "https://www.youtube.com/@stephcurry/videos",
        



          19
                "order_by": "",
        



          20
                "num_of_posts": "",
        



          21
              },
        



          22
            }
        



          23
          
        



          24
            spiderInfoJSON, err := json.Marshal(spiderInfo)
        



          25
            if err != nil {
        



          26
              fmt.Println("Error marshaling JSON:", err)
        



          27
              return
        



          28
            }
        



          29
            
        



          30
            formData := url.Values{}
        



          31
            formData.Add("spider_name", "youtube.com")
        



          32
            formData.Add("spider_id", "youtube_video-post_by-url")
        



          33
            formData.Add("spider_parameters", string(spiderInfoJSON))
        



          34
            formData.Add("spider_errors", "true")
        



          35
            formData.Add("file_name", "{{VideoID}}")
        



          36
            
        



          37
            req, err := http.NewRequest("POST", targetURL, strings.NewReader(formData.Encode()))
        



          38
            if err != nil {
        



          39
              fmt.Println("Error creating request:", err)
        



          40
              return
        



          41
            }
        



          42
          
        



          43
            req.Header.Set("Authorization", "Bearer Token")
        



          44
            req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
        



          45
          
        



          46
            resp, err := client.Do(req)
        



          47
            if err != nil {
        



          48
              fmt.Println("Error sending request:", err)
        



          49
              return
        



          50
            }
        



          51
            defer resp.Body.Close()
        



          52
          
        



          53
            body, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
        



          54
            if err != nil {
        



          55
              fmt.Println("Error reading response:", err)
        



          56
              return
        



          57
            }
        



          58
          
        



          59
            fmt.Printf("Status Code: %d\n", resp.StatusCode)
        



          60
            fmt.Printf("Response Body: %s\n", body)
        



          61
          }

          4
          import java.net.http.HttpResponse;
        



          5
          import java.net.URLEncoder;
        



          6
          import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
        



          7
          import java.util.Map;
        



          8
          import java.util.stream.Collectors;
        



          9
          
        



          10
          public class Main {
        



          11
              public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception {
        



          12
                  String formData = buildFormData(
        



          13
                      "url", "https://www.google.com",
        



          14
                      "type", "html",
        



          15
                      "js_render", "True"
        



          16
              );
        



          17
                  
        



          18
                  HttpRequest request = HttpRequest.newBuilder()
        



          19
                          .uri(URI.create("https://webunlocker.thordata.com/request"))
        



          20
                          .header("Authorization", "Bearer Token")
        



          21
                          .header("content-type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
        



          22
                          .POST(HttpRequest.BodyPublishers.ofString(formData))
        



          23
                          .build();
        



          24
                  
        



          25
                  HttpResponse<String> response = HttpClient.newHttpClient()
        



          26
                          .send(request, HttpResponse.BodyHandlers.ofString());
        



          27
                  
        



          28
                  System.out.println(response.body());
        



          29
              }
        



          30
              
        



          31
              
        



          32
              private static String buildFormData(String... keyValuePairs) {
        



          33
                  if (keyValuePairs.length % 2 != 0) {
        



          34
                      throw new IllegalArgumentException("Parameters must be key-value pairs.");
        



          35
                  }
        



          36
                  
        



          37
                  StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        



          38
                  for (int i = 0; i < keyValuePairs.length; i += 2) {
        



          39
                      if (i > 0) sb.append("&");
        



          40
                      sb.append(keyValuePairs[i])
        



          41
                        .append("=")
        



          42
                        .append(URLEncoder.encode(keyValuePairs[i+1], StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
        



          43
                  }
        



          44
                  return sb.toString();
        



          45
              }
        



          46
          }

          12

          14

          17
            
        



          18

          19

          import java.io.*;
        



          2
          import java.net.HttpURLConnection;
        



          3
          import java.net.URL;
        



          4
          import java.net.URLEncoder;
        



          5
          import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
        



          6
          import java.util.ArrayList;
        



          7
          import java.util.HashMap;
        



          8
          import java.util.List;
        



          9
          import java.util.Map;
        



          10
          
        



          11
          public class Main {
        



          12
            public static void main(String[] args) {
        



          13
              String targetURL = "https://scraperapi.thordata.com/builder";
        



          14
          
        



          15
              // Create the spider info data structure
        



          16
              List<Map<String, String>> spiderInfo = new ArrayList<>();
        



          17
              
        



          18
              Map<String, String> param1 = new HashMap<>();
        



          19
                param1.put("url", "https://www.youtube.com/@stephcurry/videos");
        



          20
                param1.put("order_by", "");
        



          21
                param1.put("num_of_posts", "");
        



          22
                spiderInfo.add(param1);
        



          23
          
        



          24
              
        



          25
              try {
        



          26
                // Convert spiderInfo to JSON
        



          27
                String spiderInfoJSON = new com.google.gson.Gson().toJson(spiderInfo);
        



          28
                
        



          29
                // Prepare form data
        



          30
                String formData = "spider_name=" + URLEncoder.encode("youtube.com", StandardCharsets.UTF_8) + 
        



          31
                "&spider_id=" + URLEncoder.encode("youtube_video-post_by-url", StandardCharsets.UTF_8) + 
        



          32
                "&spider_parameters=" + URLEncoder.encode(spiderInfoJSON, StandardCharsets.UTF_8) + 
        



          33
                "&spider_errors=" + URLEncoder.encode("true", StandardCharsets.UTF_8) + 
        



          34
                "&file_name=" + URLEncoder.encode("{{VideoID}}", StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
        



          35
          
        



          36
                // Create and configure the connection
        



          37
                HttpURLConnection connection = (HttpURLConnection) new URL(targetURL).openConnection();
        



          38
                connection.setRequestMethod("POST");
        



          39
                connection.setRequestProperty("Authorization", "Bearer Token");
        



          40
                connection.setRequestProperty("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded");
        



          41
                connection.setDoOutput(true);
        



          42
          
        



          43
                // Send the request
        



          44
                try (OutputStream os = connection.getOutputStream()) {
        



          45
                  byte[] input = formData.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8);
        



          46
                  os.write(input, 0, input.length);
        



          47
                }
        



          48
          
        



          49
                // Get the response
        



          50
                int statusCode = connection.getResponseCode();
        



          51
                String responseBody;
        



          52
                try (InputStream is = connection.getInputStream();
        



          53
                  BufferedReader br = new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(is, StandardCharsets.UTF_8))) {
        



          54
                  StringBuilder response = new StringBuilder();
        



          55
                  String responseLine;
        



          56
                  while ((responseLine = br.readLine()) != null) {
        



          57
                    response.append(responseLine.trim());
        



          58
                  }
        



          59
                  responseBody = response.toString();
        



          60
                }
        



          61
          
        



          62
                System.out.println("Status Code: " + statusCode);
        



          63
                System.out.println("Response Body: " + responseBody);
        



          64
          
        



          65
              } catch (Exception e) {
        



          66
                System.err.println("Error: " + e.getMessage());
        



          67
                e.printStackTrace();
        



          68
              }
        



          69
            }
        



          70
          }

          21

          22
                          .POST(HttpRequest.BodyPublishers.ofString(formData))
        



          23
                          .build();
        



          24

          29
              }
        



          30
              
        



          31
              
        



          32
              private static String buildFormData(String... keyValuePairs) {
        



          33
                  if (keyValuePairs.length % 2 != 0) {
        



          34
                      throw new IllegalArgumentException("Parameters must be key-value pairs.");
        



          35
                  }
        



          36
                  
        



          37
                  StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();
        



          38
                  for (int i = 0; i < keyValuePairs.length; i += 2) {
        



          39
                      if (i > 0) sb.append("&");
        



          40
                      sb.append(keyValuePairs[i])
        



          41
                        .append("=")
        



          42
                        .append(URLEncoder.encode(keyValuePairs[i+1], StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
        



          43
                  }
        



          44
                  return sb.toString();
        



          45
              }
        



          46
          }

Web Scraper API
# Web Scraper APIs
Dedicated endpoints for real-time, structured data from 120+ domains—skip the scraping infrastructure and integrate seamlessly.
Scrape on demand via API or no-code scraper
Only pay for successfully delivered results
Bulk request handling, up to 10K URLs
Structured data output (JSON, CSV, XLSX)
Contact sales[Start free trial](https://dashboard.thordata.com/register)
![thordata.com](https://www.thordata.com/img/v2/webscraper/apis/banner.webp)
## Web Scraper API crawling methods
### API Scraping
Call the API to start scraping specified data.
#### Build your request with our API
Large-scale web scraping via API, supports 9 programming languages.
#### Functional automation
Create a custom scraping scheduler tailored to your specific requirements.
#### Data delivery
Automatically deliver scraped data to your designated cloud storage service.
### No-Code Scraping
Use the dashboard panel to directly scrape specified data.
#### Dashboard-based scraping tool
The entire process is managed within our [Dashboard - Web Scraper] control panel.
#### Flexible & user-friendly
Configure targets and start scraping instantly with scheduled tasks for automated collection.
#### Retrieve results
Download output files directly from the [Dashboard - Tasks].
## Web Scraper API store
Pre-configured templates for Amazon, YouTube, LinkedIn and 120+ sites—delivering accurate, efficient data extraction with zero setup.

Shell
```


          1
          curl -X POST "https://scraperapi.thordata.com/video_builder" \
        



          2
            -H 'Authorization: Bearer API_TOKEN' \
        



          3
            -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
        



          4
            -d 'spider_name=youtube.com' \
        



          5
            -d 'spider_id=youtube_video_by-url' \
        



          6
            -d 'spider_parameters=[{"url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_SdpvpvVrLY"},{"url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8RePenzQH80"}]' \
        



          7
            -d 'spider_universal={"resolution": "2160p","is_subtitles": "true","subtitles_language": "en"}' \
        



          8
            -d 'spider_errors=true'\
        



          9
            -d 'file_name={{VideoID}}'
        


```

## Frequently asked questions
### What is a Web Scraper API?
A Web Scraper API is a cloud-based service that simplifies web data extraction. It automates IP rotation, CAPTCHA solving, and data parsing into structured formats—enabling efficient, scalable data collection for seamless integration into business workflows.
### Who can benefit from using a Web Scraper API?
Data analysts, scientists, engineers, and developers who need to efficiently collect and analyze web data for AI, ML, big data applications, and beyond.
### Why choose a Web Scraper API over manual scraping?
It overcomes key limitations of manual scraping: handling website changes, avoiding blocks and CAPTCHAs, and reducing infrastructure costs. The API provides an automated, scalable, and reliable solution—cutting operational effort and time.
### How do I get started?
Start quickly through dashboard, which offers clear documentation and an intuitive dashboard for API key and setting management. This streamlined setup lets you begin extracting data right away with a highly reliable and scalable platform.
### Can I automate recurring scraping jobs with Web Scraper API?
Yes, with the free Scheduler feature, you can automate recurring scraping jobs by scheduling them. Simply put, you don't need to send new requests with identical parameters to receive regular updates. Also, there's no need to create or maintain your scheduling scripts.
### How does it handle large-scale extraction?
The API supports high concurrency and batch processing, enabling efficient large-scale scraping with high throughput—so you can manage massive request volumes without compromising speed or reliability.
### How long does it take to get results from the Web Scraper API?
The API delivers real-time results from most websites globally. Delivery times vary based on the target site's complexity and load. For precise estimates on specific sites, contact your Account Manager or our support team.
### Is web scraping legal?
Web scraping can be legal if conducted in compliance with applicable laws and website terms. We recommend reviewing our detailed blog post on this topic and consulting legal counsel before starting any scraping project to mitigate risks.

## Frequently asked questions
### What is SERP API?
SERP (Search Engine Results Page) is the ranked page displayed after a web search, containing organic search results sorted by relevance, paid advertisements, and other content. Since users typically only click on results from the first page, the core goal of SEO (Search Engine Optimization) is to improve a website’s ranking on SERP to gain more traffic.
### What is SERP API used for?
SERP API is designed for SEO monitoring, helping businesses track keyword rankings, analyze search trends, and optimize online visibility. This interface also efficiently saves market research time, aiding companies in gathering competitive intelligence, monitoring ad performance, and analyzing SERP features like featured snippets and local packs. Additionally, you can use SERP extraction API to track paid search performance and optimize ad placement strategies.
### Is SERP API suitable for SEO analysis?
It is a powerful tool that enables businesses to extract precise structured data directly from search engine results pages (SERP). Whether you are an SEO professional monitoring keyword performance or a marketer seeking competitive intelligence, the Google search engine results interface can be your secret weapon.
### What is the core difference between Google and SERP?
Google is a search engine company that uses algorithms to crawl, index web content, and process user search queries. 
SERP (Search Engine Results Page) is the final page users see when using search engines like Google, containing modular content such as organic search results, paid ads, featured snippets, and local packs.
### Is SERP API legal?
The legality of SERP scraping depends on multiple factors, including the terms of use and service restrictions of the scraped websites. When using SERP API, it is essential to pay special attention to legality and compliance, ensuring that scraping activities adhere to search engine guidelines and avoiding excessively frequent requests that may trigger anti-scraping mechanisms.

## Automated web unlocking, seamless data collection
#### Intelligent anti-bot bypass
Simulates browser fingerprints and behaviors to effectively evade anti-scraping detection.
#### Fully automated CAPTCHA resolution
Automatically solves CAPTCHAs with zero manual intervention required.
#### Automatic retries & global IP rotation
Leverages a tens-of-millions-strong IP pool to ensure high request success rates, eliminating manual proxy management.
#### Seamless JS rendering support
Fully renders JavaScript-heavy websites to capture dynamic content.
#### Pay only for success
You are only charged for successful data retrieval; invalid responses are completely free.
#### Simple integration
Provides ready-to-use code examples in multiple languages for easy workflow integration.

## Frequently asked questions
### What is the Web Unlocker?
The Thordata Web Unlocker is a high-performance public data extraction tool that automates the scraping of website data. It integrates intelligent proxy management and anti-bot bypass capabilities, enabling stable and efficient collection of structured data globally.
### What anti-bot mechanisms can the Web Unlocker handle?
Thordata’s Web Unlocker automatically identifies and counteracts common anti-bot strategies, including:
CAPTCHA recognition
Dynamic JavaScript rendering
Browser fingerprinting
IP blocking
### Why choose Thordata’s Web Unlocker?
Higher Success Rate: Intelligent IP rotation and request retry mechanisms ensure over 99% success rates.
Maintenance-Free: No need to build or manage proxy pools or anti-bot systems.
Compatible with All Websites: Supports scraping e-commerce, media, social media, travel, recruitment, and other types of websites.
Highly Scalable: Adapts to multi-language development environments.
### Is using the Web Unlocker legal?
The service is legal in most scenarios. However, users must comply with relevant laws, regulations, and the target website’s terms of service. Thordata advises users to only scrape publicly accessible data, strictly adhere to website rules, and avoid activities that infringe on privacy or intellectual property rights.
### What advantages does Thordata’s Web Unlocker offer over building a system in-house?
Compared to in-house solutions, Thordata’s Web Unlocker provides the following benefits:
No maintenance of proxy pools or anti-bot mechanisms required
Automatic handling of IP rotation and failed requests
Lower costs with higher stability
Faster deployment and dedicated technical support
